title: Chapter 5: The Parking Garage
content: The stairwell smelled like rust and regret.

Marcus pressed himself against the wall, fingers white-knuckled around the crowbar he'd found in the maintenance closet. The crowbar was cold and heavy and absolutely useless in his hands—he'd never fought anything stronger than a lag switch—but holding it felt better than holding nothing.

Ahead of him, Sera moved like smoke. Silent, fluid, her bare feet making no sound on the concrete steps. She'd discarded her torn jacket back at the apartment, leaving the sleeveless blouse that showed arms corded with muscle that hadn't been there before the Spirit Meat. Evolution was sculpting her into something between human and weapon.
She paused at the door to B1. Her head tilted. Listening.
"Two heartbeats," she murmured. "One fast. One slow. The fast one is afraid. The slow one is... calm. Too calm."
"Which is which?"

"The calm one is the man. He's in control. The afraid one is the woman." Sera's jaw tightened. "I know what that sounds like. I've heard it in boardrooms. Meetings where someone with power made someone without it very, very uncomfortable."
She pushed open the door.

The parking garage was a tomb of vehicles. Most were wrecks—windshields shattered, doors hanging open, interiors smeared with the brown streaks of old violence. The overhead fluorescents were dead, leaving only the gray daylight filtering through the ramp entrance and the occasional glint of Sera's eyes.
Voices echoed from the far end.
"—told you to stay quiet! Do you want to bring every dead thing in the city down here?"
A woman's voice, strained: "Derek, I can't just—my leg—"
"Your leg is *fine*. You're being dramatic. Like always."
Marcus crept between the cars, Sera a shadow at his flank. Through a shattered rear window, he saw them.

Derek Cole. Six-one, broad shoulders, the kind of jawline that belonged on a cologne ad and the kind of eyes that gave you the feeling he'd calculated your net worth before saying hello. He wore a designer jacket over a henley, somehow still looking put-together in the apocalypse, and he sat on the hood of the only intact SUV in the garage with the lazy confidence of a man who'd never doubted his place in the food chain.

Elena Reyes. Five-six, dark hair pulled back in a practical knot, a dust-stained scrub top that marked her as medical staff—no, nursing student, the System had said. She sat on the concrete floor beside the SUV, one hand pressed to her calf where a bandage was wrapped too tight and stained with old blood. Dark circles under her eyes. The kind of exhaustion that wasn't just physical.

Derek was eating. A protein bar—scarce, valuable, the kind of thing survivors killed for. He was breaking off pieces and eating them slowly, deliberately, while Elena watched. He didn't offer her any.
"System," Marcus whispered, "analysis."

**[DEREK COLE — Threat Assessment]**
**[Physical threat: Moderate (stronger than you, armed with crowbar)]**
**[Psychological threat: HIGH (manipulative, controlling, physically abusive based on stress patterns)]**
**[Notable: Bite scratch on left shoulder, concealed under jacket. Infection status: UNKNOWN]**
**[Probability he'll attack you on sight: 78%]**

**[ELENA REYES — Status Assessment]**
**[Physical condition: Exhausted, mildly injured (calf laceration, healing poorly)]**
**[Psychological condition: Trapped. Fear conditioning. She won't run because she believes she can't survive alone.]**
**[Medical knowledge: Valuable—nursing background]**
**[Probability she'll trust you immediately: 12%]**
**[Probability she'll trust you after witnessing Derek's true nature: 64%]**
Twelve percent. Great. She'd sooner trust the zombies.
Sera touched his arm. Her fingers were cold as ever, but the gesture was warm. "Let me go first," she breathed. "He'll see you as prey. He'll see me as... a distraction."
"A distraction?"
"I was a distraction in board meetings too. The most powerful piece on the board is the one everyone underestimates." Her lips curved. "Watch."
She stepped out from behind the car.

Derek's head snapped toward her. His hand went to the crowbar at his hip— then stopped. Froze. His eyes widened as he took in Sera: the beauty, the paleness, the grey eyes, the stillness that was too perfect for anything alive.
"What the *fuck*—" He scrambled off the hood, crowbar in hand, backing toward Elena. "It's one of them! It's a—"
"I'm not here for you," Sera said. Her voice was level, eerily calm, and the sound of coherent speech from something that looked dead made Derek's face twist in visceral horror.
"She *talks*? That's not—they don't *talk*—"
"Some of us do." Sera took a step forward. Just one. "I'm here for the woman. Is she yours?"
Derek positioned himself between Sera and Elena, crowbar raised. Marcus noted the strategy—putting his body between the threat and the "asset." Not protection. Possession.
"She's with me," Derek said. "Who the hell are you? What are you?"

"I'm the thing that's going to eat your face if you don't answer my question properly." Sera smiled. It was the same thin, predator smile Marcus had seen when she'd faced the Howlers. "Is. She. Yours."
Elena's voice came from behind Derek, thin but present: "I'm not anyone's property."
"Shut up, Elena." Derek didn't look at her. His eyes were locked on Sera with the desperate focus of a man realizing he was no longer the apex predator.
Marcus stepped out.
He kept the crowbar visible but not raised. Let Derek see him—regular guy, average build, visibly nervous. Unthreatening.
"Hey," Marcus said. "I live upstairs. I heard voices."
Derek's gaze flicked between Marcus and Sera, reassessing. A guy with a crowbar he could handle. A talking zombie? Not in any playbook.
"You're alive," Derek said, as if this were an accusation.
"Last I checked. Look, I don't want trouble. I just—"

"You have food." Derek's eyes were on Marcus's jacket pocket, where the slight bulge of the bottled water was visible. His pupils dilated. Hunger—not the zombie kind, but close. "You have *supplies*. I can tell. You don't look like you're starving."
"I have enough for me and—" Marcus gestured at Sera. "—her."
"Her?" Derek let out a brittle laugh. "You're keeping a *pet zombie*? Are you insane?"
"She's not a pet." The words came out sharper than Marcus intended. "She's my companion."
The word hung in the oil-stained air of the garage. *Companion*. Not pet, not weapon, not tool. Companion.
Sera's glance at him was brief but luminous. Something flickered in those grey eyes that looked a lot like warmth.

Elena had risen from the floor, favoring her injured leg. She peered around Derek's shoulder at Marcus—at his relatively healthy appearance, at the crowbar held loosely at his side, at the obvious fact that he'd been surviving better than she had.
"You have a safe place?" she asked. "Upstairs?"
"Elena," Derek said, voice hard. "We're not going anywhere with the zombie-fucker."

But Elena wasn't looking at Derek. She was looking at Marcus, and in her eyes Marcus saw something he recognized from his own mirror: the calculation of desperation. The mental math of *is this better than what I have*.

**[CONQUEST QUEST UNLOCKED — ELENA REYES]**
**[QUEST: The Knight in Rusted Armor]**
**[Elena Reyes is trapped in an abusive dynamic. She won't leave until she believes she has a better option.]**
**[Objective: Demonstrate that you are the better option.]**
**[Method: Not words. Actions. Show her safety. Show her food. Show her respect.]**
**[Bonus: Expose Derek's dark secret—the scratch he's hiding, the companion he already sacrificed.]**
**[Ultimate Reward: Titanium Safehouse (Fortress-grade shelter, self-sustaining)]**
**[Time Limit: 48 hours before Derek's infection status forces his hand]**
"System," Marcus muttered under his breath, "why does every quest read like a therapist's nightmare?"

**[Romance requires trust. Trust requires safety. Safety requires effort.]**
**[Effort requires motivation. Motivation requires reward.]**
**[The System provides the reward. You provide the effort.]**
**[Fair exchange.]**
Forty-eight hours. A titanium fortress. A woman who needed rescuing.
And a scumbag who was going to learn what happened when you kept treating people like resources in a world where the resources had teeth.
"I'll be back," Marcus said. "With food. Real food. And you—" He looked at Elena directly. "You can come with me. Both of you. But the offer's open, and it won't expire."
Derek's crowbar twitched. "We don't need your charity."
Marcus was already turning away. But Elena's voice caught him at the door.
"When?" she asked. "When will you be back?"
"Tomorrow."
He didn't see her face, but he heard Derek hiss something at her—a low, venomous "What the hell are you doing?"
And he heard her answer, quiet and stubborn: "Surviving."
Sera fell into step beside him as they climbed the stairs. "You're going to take his woman."
"I'm going to give her a choice. He already took that away."
Sera studied him for a long moment. Then: "You're a strange human, Marcus Chen."
"Takes one to know one. Takes a, uh... former one."
The ghost of a smile. "I'm going to hold you to 'tomorrow.' And Marcus?"
"Yeah?"
"When you come back—bring the big guns. Metaphorically. I have a feeling Derek Cole doesn't give up what he considers his without a fight."
She wasn't wrong.

**[QUEST ACCEPTED: The Knight in Rusted Armor]**
**[Timer: 47:58:22]**
The clock was ticking. And somewhere in the distance, the Alpha's call echoed through the dead city.
